channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Misinformation about California fires is spreading like, well, wildfire
item.description: The internet has exploded with fake images of the Hollywood sign burning, conspiracy theories about satanic rituals, and misinformation about government property seizures.
item.dc:creator: Jason Henry, The Mercury News

item.title: AI deepfakes estimated to cause $40 billion in losses by 2027
item.description: Blackberry’s Global Threat Intelligence report reveals critical infrastructure and deepfake attacks are on the rise.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The Ultimate Guide to Android File Management
item.description: Discover the best file managers for Android, including Google Files and Solid Explorer, and learn how to manage your files on-the-go with these expert tips.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Biden Issues Executive Order to Strengthen Cybersecurity
item.description: President Joe Biden issued an executive order aimed at strengthening the nation's cybersecurity and making it easier to go after foreign adversaries or hacking groups that try to compromise U.S. internet and telecommunication systems.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How Gen AI enhances data governance initiatives
item.description: Discover how generative AI streamlines data governance, enhancing trust, security, and business-driven data adoption.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Build API clients with Microsoft Kiota
item.description: Service-oriented architectures are at the heart of modern application development. By building applications out of a mix of services, we can take advantage of the current generation of platforms to build serverless, scalable, distributed applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Chief Data Officer: sotto i riflettori nella veste di strateghi aziendali
item.description: Con i dati ormai al centro di ogni aspetto del business, il Chief Data Officer [in inglese] è diventato un dirigente altamente strategico che, oggi, si concentra nell’aiutare l’azienda a sfruttare le informazioni come risorsa imprescindibile per ottenere risultati.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: From safety to accountability: the game-changing impact of Kazakhstan’s surveillance system
item.description: Technology innovations have made the digital transformation of public services essential to enhancing safety, efficiency, and accountability.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 5 things to know about TikTok replacement RedNote
item.description: Earlier this week, the Chinese app Xiaohongshu, known as RedNote, shot up to become the most-downloaded app on the Apple’s U.S. App Store, partly as a “joke’s on you” moment to the American government as well as the desire for a replacement app ahead of the incoming TikTok ban.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Joe Biden going out same way as Aaron Rodgers
item.description: Bill O'Reilly believes outgoing president Joe Biden is "going out the same way" as New York Jets’ starting quarterback Aaron Rodgers.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about RedNote, a TikTok alternative
item.description: TikTok patrons are turning to a new platform in light of the nationwide ban set to shut down the app on Sunday. RedNote, a Chinese short-form video platform, is gaining ground as users look for an alternative to the social media company owned by the China's ByteDance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Americans are using the Chinese RedNote app to express their frustration with the TikTok ban
item.description: The Supreme Court and the U.S. government seem hell-bent on pushing TikTok out of existence in the United States in the next week—at least unless its Chinese-based parent company, ByteDance, accedes to a fire sale to a suitable buyer based closer to home.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Chinese cars are close to a US ban – and that could make EVs a lot more expensive
item.description: As part of a crackdown on vehicle software and hardware from China, the Biden Administration has finalized its rules for banning Chinese EVs in the US market.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia abandons the problematic 12VHPWR RTX 4080 power connector with a much longer one for the RTX 5080 FE according to leaked images
item.description: Nvidia's made me happy with its DLSS 4 generosity and affordable RTX 5070 price, and that's rolled on with the new and improved RTX 5080 power adapter.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: What the tech industry wants from the Trump administration and Congress
item.description: The technology industry is calling on the incoming Trump administration and Congress to create the necessary tax and regulatory environment. The post What the tech industry wants from the Trump administration and Congress first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: 5 ways to avoid AI in Windows
item.description: Generative AI (genAI) is seemingly everywhere these days — and Windows is certainly no exception. The OS itself now has Copilot on its taskbar, and new PCs ship with a dedicated Copilot AI key on their keyboards.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Biden signs ambitious order to bolster energy resources for AI data centers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Joe Biden on Tuesday signed an ambitious executive order on artificial intelligence that seeks to ensure the infrastructure needed for advanced AI operations, such as large-scale data centers and new clean power facilities, can be built quickly and at scale in the United
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Court lets Tennessee porn site age verification law take effect as Texas law goes to Supreme Court
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A panel of appeals judges has ruled that Tennessee can begin enforcing a law that requires pornographic websites to verify their visitors’ age as the First Amendment debate rises to the U.S. Supreme Court this week for oral arguments over a similar Texas law.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Britain's leader unveils AI strategy, including vow to rip up red tape hindering AI projects
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The British government pledged on Monday to rip up the red tape hindering construction of data centers that underpin artificial intelligence while promoting its “pro-innovation” approach to regulating the technology.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CISA aims to help cyber, tech pros find their path
item.description: CISA’s National Initiative for Cybersecurity Careers and Studies helps cyber pros keep up with technology changes ranging from AI to zero trust. The post CISA aims to help cyber, tech pros find their path first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: PowerSchool data breach exposes millions of student and teacher records
item.description: PowerSchool, which serves over 60 million students and teachers globally, has become the latest target of cybercriminals. Millions of records were stolen.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Best cutting-edge health and fitness tech from CES 2025
item.description: CES 2025 showcased everything from artificial intelligence-powered sleep aids to smart ear-cleaning devices. Kurt the CyberGuy has the details.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prime Video's use of AI, Next Gen Stats on NFL games is helping viewers understand the game better
item.description: CULVER CITY, Calif. (AP) — The Los Angeles Rams defense appeared to be content to drop back in coverage and prevent San Francisco’s Brock Purdy from completing a Hail Mary on the final play of their Thursday night game on Dec. 12. The broadcast on Amazon Prime Video saw it differently, though.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: TikTok gets frosty reception at Supreme Court in fight to stave off ban
item.description: TikTok received a frosty reception in its fight to save the platform at the Supreme Court, which during oral arguments Friday expressed sympathy with the government’s national security concerns about the platform’s ties to China.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Five takeaways from GSA’s latest Section 508 assessment
item.description: Agencies are dedicating more time and staff to digital accessibility efforts, but acquisition is still a key hurdle when it comes to Section 508 compliance. The post Five takeaways from GSA’s latest Section 508 assessment first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Nvidia year in review
item.description: 2024 was Nvidia’s year. Its command of mindshare and market share was unequaled among tech vendors. Here’s a recap of some of the key events of 2024 that highlight just how powerful the world’s most dominant chip player is.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
